title: Brass's Question on Unit Distances in Normed Planes
statement_text: Does there exist a $2$-norm $\|.\|$ with $U_{\|.\|}(n)\leq O(n\log n)$?
context_text: Brass posed this question in 1996 when studying the unit distance problem in normed spaces. At the time, the best known upper bound for typical norms was Matoušek's $O(n\log n\log\log n)$, and it was open whether the extra $\log\log n$ factor could be removed even for some specific norm.
paper_contribution: Proves that for almost all $d$-norms $\|.\|$ on $\mathbb{R}^d$ (in a Baire categorical sense), any set of $n$ points determines at most $\frac{1}{2}d\cdot n\log_2 n$ unit distances, answering the question affirmatively for almost all $2$-norms.
paper_title: Unit and distinct distances in typical norms

title: Brass-Moser-Pach Problems 4 and 5 on Unit Distances in Higher Dimensions
statement_text: For every $d\geq 3$ and every $d$-norm $\|.\|$, is $U_{\|.\|}(n)$ asymptotically at least $\Omega(n\log n)$?
context_text: Brass, Moser and Pach listed these as Problems 4 and 5 in Chapter 5 (p. 195) of their book on research problems in discrete geometry, conjecturing that the $\Omega(n\log n)$ lower bound on unit distances holds universally for all norms in dimension $d\geq 3$.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every $d$-norm $\|.\|$ on $\mathbb{R}^d$ and any sufficiently large $n$, one can find $n$ points determining at least $\frac{1}{2}(d-1-o(1))\cdot n\log_2 n$ unit distances, confirming the $\Omega(n\log n)$ lower bound for all norms in all dimensions $d\geq 2$.
notes: Introduction excerpt cuts off before the complete statement; kind inferred from 'Problems 4 and 5' phrasing and surrounding text.
paper_title: Unit and distinct distances in typical norms
